expressions.expression: get the monkey off your back
expressions.dialogue:
Commercial Voiceover: "**Can't get the monkey off your back?** Then put it in your mouth... With MonkeyShine Beer. MonkeyShine Beer, 'cause it's a jungle out there."
Ross: "That commercial always makes me so sad."
Joey: "Yeah, but then the guy opens his beer and those girls run at him, so, everything seems to work out OK."
expressions.speaker: Commercial Voiceover
expressions.meaning: 오랫동안 괴롭혀 온 부담이나 문제를 없애다
expressions.nuance: 원래 마약 중독 맥락에서 나온 표현이지만 지금은 어떤 골치 아픈 짐에도 쓰임. 이 장면에서는 원숭이 테마 광고가 패러디로 이 표현을 활용함.
expressions.example: "I finally paid off my student loans. I feel like I **got the monkey off my back**." → "드디어 학자금 대출을 다 갚았어. 오랜 짐을 내려놓은 느낌이야."

expressions.dialogue:
Commercial Voiceover: "Can't get the monkey off your back? Then put it in your mouth... With MonkeyShine Beer. **'Cause it's a jungle out there.**"
Ross: "That commercial always makes me so sad."
Joey: "Yeah, but then the guy opens his beer and those girls run at him, so, everything seems to work out OK."
expressions.speaker: Commercial Voiceover
expressions.meaning: 세상은 험하고 살아남기 힘들다
expressions.nuance: 자연의 약육강식을 세상의 혹독함에 비유한 관용구. 가볍게도, 진지하게도 쓸 수 있음. TV 드라마 Monk의 주제가 제목이기도 해서 미국 문화에서 자주 접함.
expressions.example: "You need to negotiate your salary hard at this company. **It's a jungle out there.**" → "이 회사에서는 연봉 협상을 강하게 해야 해. 세상 참 험해."

expressions.dialogue:
Ross: "That commercial always makes me so sad."
Joey: "Yeah, but then the guy opens his beer and those girls run at him, so, **everything seems to work out OK**."
Ross: "I meant because the monkey in it reminds me of Marcel."
Phoebe: "I can see that, 'cause they both have those big brown eyes and, ya know, the little pouty chin."
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 결국에는 다 잘 풀리다
expressions.nuance: "work out"은 "운동하다" 외에 "잘 해결되다"는 의미로 구어에서 매우 자주 쓰임. Joey가 피상적인 맥락을 진지하게 해석하는 개그이기도 함. "things work out", "it all worked out"도 같은 패턴.
expressions.example: "I was really worried about the presentation, but **everything worked out OK** in the end." → "발표가 정말 걱정됐는데, 결국 다 잘 됐어."

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Can we, can we just stop for a second? Who said something better would come along, huh? You didn't believe me. I told you everything was gonna work out. (gasps) You know what? This calls for a bottle of Israels finest.

expressions.dialogue:
Monica: [reading] '...enclosed please find 14 of my eyelashes.'
Rachel: "**Ya know, in crazy world, that means you're married.**"
Monica: "This wasn't addressed to Days of Our Lives, this came to your apartment. There's no stamp on it, this woman was in our building."
Joey: "Oh my god, I got my very own stalker."
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: "(어떤 이상한 논리 속에서라면) 그건 ~라는 뜻이야"
expressions.nuance: 특정 사람이나 집단의 비정상적인 논리를 가정할 때 쓰는 구어 표현. "in his world", "in fan world", "in dating world" 등으로 변형 가능. 살짝 비꼬는 뉘앙스가 있음.
expressions.example: "He texted back three hours later with just 'lol'. **In his world**, that probably counts as flirting." → "세 시간 후에 'lol'이라고만 답장 왔어. 그 사람 논리로는 그게 플러팅이겠지."

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Okay, okay. If I were omnipotent for a day, I would want, um, world peace, no more hunger, good things for the rain-forest...And bigger boobs!

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: (to Phoebe) I hit her in the eye! I hit her in the eye! This is the worst break-up in the history of the world.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: In Bizarro World!! You broke the code!

expressions.dialogue:
[Central Perk]
Joey: "Hey, hey, **check it out**, guess what I got."
Chandler: "Rhythm?"
Joey: "No, my first fan mail."
All: "Alright!"
Monica: [reading] 'Dear Dr. Remore...'
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: "이것 좀 봐", "들어봐" — 무언가를 보여주거나 알릴 때 쓰는 캐주얼한 표현
expressions.nuance: 사물을 직접 보여줄 때뿐 아니라 흥미로운 소식을 전할 때도 씀. 1990년대부터 지금까지 꾸준히 쓰이는 매우 자연스러운 구어 표현.
expressions.example: "**Check it out** — the new season just dropped on Netflix." → "이거 봐봐 — 넷플릭스에 새 시즌 방금 나왔어."

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: (to Ross) Hey! Here's the birthday boy! Ross, check it out: hockey tickets, Rangers-Penguins, tonight at the Garden, and we're taking you.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Check it out, Giants-Cowboys. (He has a pocket TV)

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Check it out, check it out. Heckles' high school yearbook.

expressions.dialogue:
Monica: [reading] 'Dear Dr. Remore, know that I love you and would do anything to have you. **Your not-so-secretive admirer**, Erica Ford.' Ooh wait, 'PS enclosed please find 14 of my eyelashes.'
Rachel: "Ya know, in crazy world, that means you're married."
Monica: "This wasn't addressed to Days of Our Lives, this came to your apartment."
expressions.speaker: Monica (reading the letter)
expressions.meaning: "그다지 ~하지 않은" — 형용사 앞에 붙여 역설적인 의미를 강조하는 패턴
expressions.nuance: "secret admirer(몰래 짝사랑하는 사람)"를 비틀어 "전혀 비밀스럽지 않은 짝사랑"으로 만든 유머. "not-so-subtle", "not-so-great", "not-so-innocent" 등 다양하게 변형 가능.
expressions.example: "She gave me a **not-so-subtle** hint that she wanted flowers for her birthday." → "생일에 꽃을 원한다는 걸 전혀 숨기지 않고 드러냈어."

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: There's nothing to tell! He's just some guy I work with!

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Um, not so good. He walked me to the subway and said 'We should do this again!'

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: No, I know, but it's just so hard, you know? I mean, you're sitting there with her, she has no idea what's happening, and then you finally get up the courage to do it, and there's the horrible awkward moment when you've handed her the note.

expressions.dialogue:
Ross: "Hey guys."
All: "Hey."
Phoebe: "Ooh, **where are you off to**, Travelin' Jake?"
Ross: "Well, there's this, uh, paleontology conference in L.A. so I figured I'd go and then drive down to the zoo and surprise Marcel."
Chandler: "You know I think he will be surprised, 'till he realizes he's a monkey..."
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: "어디 가는 길이야?" — 누군가가 막 이동하려는 상황에서 묻는 친근한 표현
expressions.nuance: "Where are you going?"보다 훨씬 구어적이고 자연스러운 표현. 특히 상대가 나가려는 참에 물어볼 때 잘 어울리며, 약간 가볍고 다정한 어감이 있음.
expressions.example: "**Where are you off to** so early? It's barely 7am." → "이렇게 일찍 어디 가는 거야? 겨우 7시잖아."

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: That's great. So now, Super Gavin, when I come back where are you planning on flying off to?

expressions.dialogue:
Janitor: "**Word on the street** - well, when I say street, I mean those little pretend streets they have here at the zoo. Your monkey found a new career, in the entertainment field. That's all I know."
Ross: "This is unbelievable."
Janitor: "So, what is this information worth to you, my friend?"
Ross: "Are you trying to get me to bribe you?"
expressions.speaker: Janitor
expressions.meaning: 떠도는 소문, 입소문으로 들리는 정보
expressions.nuance: 비공식 경로로 퍼진 정보를 지칭하는 표현. "I heard through the grapevine"과 유사. 이 장면에서는 동물원 안길을 "street"라고 부르며 스스로 패러디하는 게 개그 포인트.
expressions.example: "**Word on the street** is that the boss is planning to restructure the whole team." → "소문에 의하면 팀 전체가 개편될 거래."

expressions.dialogue:
Janitor: "The zoo! Do you believe everything the zoo tells ya?"
Ross: "That's the only thing the zoo's ever told me."
Janitor: "Of course they're gonna say he's dead. They don't want the bad publicity. **It's all a great big cover-up.** Do you have any idea how high up this thing goes?"
Ross: "That guy Lipson?"
Janitor: "Lipson knows."
expressions.speaker: Janitor
expressions.meaning: 진실을 감추기 위한 조직적 은폐 (명사)
expressions.nuance: 단순 거짓말이 아니라 권력이나 조직을 이용해 진실을 숨기는 행위를 뜻함. 정치·기업 스캔들에서 자주 등장. 동사형 "cover up"("cover up the truth")과 구별해서 쓸 것.
expressions.example: "The investigation exposed a massive **cover-up** that went all the way to the top." → "조사 결과, 최고위까지 연루된 대규모 은폐가 드러났다."

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Yeah, I'm fine. (trying to cover up what he is saying) I'm trppd in an ATM vstbl wth Jll Gdcr.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Come on, show them to me. (reaches for her cards, Rachel covers them up)

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: OK, wait, wait, wait, wait. You know what? Ross, let's - let's switch places. You get in the middle. No un-, ya know, unless this looks like we're trying to cover something up.

expressions.dialogue:
Janitor: "Of course they're gonna say he's dead. They don't want the bad publicity. It's all a great big cover-up. **Do you have any idea how high up this thing goes?**"
Ross: "That guy Lipson?"
Janitor: "Lipson knows. Do you have any idea who else knows?"
Ross: "No, I only know Lipson."
Janitor: "Hmm, Lipson knows huh?"
expressions.speaker: Janitor
expressions.meaning: (비리나 음모 등이) 얼마나 높은 자리까지 연루되어 있는지
expressions.nuance: 음모론이나 부패 스캔들 맥락에서 자주 등장하는 표현. "This goes all the way to the top."과 유사. 스릴러·정치 드라마에서도 흔히 들을 수 있음.
expressions.example: "The bribery case is bigger than we thought. Nobody knows **how high up it goes** yet." → "뇌물 사건이 생각보다 훨씬 크더라. 얼마나 윗선까지 연루됐는지 아직 아무도 몰라."

expressions.expression: stick a fork in me, I'm done
expressions.dialogue:
Chandler: "I've met the perfect woman. OK, we're sitting on her couch, we're fooling around, and then suddenly she turns to me and says, 'Do you ever want to do it in an elevator?'"
Monica: "What did you say?"
Chandler: "Ahh, I believe my exact words were, 'Flaignen-sten'." ... Phoebe: "Oh, you just know."
[later] Chandler: "**Hey, stick a fork in me, I am done.**"
Phoebe: "Stick a fork what?"
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 나 완전히 다 됐어 — 지쳤거나, 결론을 내렸거나, 완전히 만족했을 때 씀
expressions.nuance: 고기 익었는지 포크로 찌르는 것에서 온 표현. "I'm done"보다 훨씬 과장되고 유머러스. Phoebe가 "포크를 뭐에?"라고 되묻는 게 이 장면의 개그 포인트.
expressions.example: "After a 12-hour shift with no break, **stick a fork in me, I'm done**." → "쉬지도 못하고 12시간 근무 끝에, 나 완전히 끝났어."

expressions.dialogue:
Rachel: "And she's not crazy?"
Chandler: "Oh no no no, **she's a total wack job.** Yeah, she thinks that Joey is actually Dr. Drake Remore."
Rachel: "Oh my God."
Monica: "Ah, and I mean, he's going out with her? He cannot pursue this."
Chandler: "Hey, just because this woman thinks she can actually see Joey through the magical box in her living room doesn't mean she's not a person."
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 완전히 정신 나간 사람 (구어, 비하 표현)
expressions.nuance: 구어에서 상대를 심하게 비하하는 슬랭. 친한 사이의 농담 외에는 조심해야 함. "nutjob", "nutcase"와 유사. 주로 1990~2000년대에 자주 쓰였고 지금도 간간이 등장.
expressions.example: "Don't listen to him, he's a **total wack job** — he thinks the government is putting chips in vaccines." → "걔 말 듣지 마, 완전 정신 나간 사람이야 — 정부가 백신에 칩을 심는다고 믿거든."

expressions.dialogue:
[On the movie set, Joey tries to sneak Phoebe away]
Joey: "OK, we'll just leave, and when we pass her on the stairs, she won't know it's me 'cause we've never met."
Chandler: "That's how radio stars escape stalkers."
Joey: "She's comin'." ...
Joey: "Well, we're just goin' over here so that we can get away from the horrible flesh eating virus, **for the love of God, woman, listen to me**. Is he lookin', is he lookin'?"
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: "제발 좀", "도대체 왜" — 절박하거나 좌절했을 때 쓰는 감탄사
expressions.nuance: 종교적 어원이 있지만 현재는 완전히 세속적인 감탄사로 쓰임. "for God's sake"와 거의 같은 의미. 강한 좌절이나 간청을 표현할 때 쓰며, 어조에 따라 유머로도 진지하게도 활용 가능.
expressions.example: "**For the love of God**, just make a decision! We've been standing here for 20 minutes." → "제발 좀, 결정 좀 내려! 우리 여기서 20분째야."

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: This is madness. It's madness, I tell you, for the love of God, Monica, don't do it!! ...Thank you.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Leave me alone! For the love of God, leave me alone!!! (Joey hangs up.)

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Oh for the love of God!

expressions.expression: What's the worst that could happen?
expressions.dialogue:
Monica: "Oh my God, Jean-Claude Van Damme. I didn't know he was in this movie, he is so hot."
Rachel: "So why don't you go talk to him?"
Monica: "Oh, yeah."
Rachel: "What, so you go over there, you tell him you think he's cute, **what's the worst that could happen?**"
Monica: "He could hear me."
Rachel: "OK, I'm doin' it for ya."
Monica: "Oh Rachel don't, don't you dare..."
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: "뭐 나쁠 게 있어?" / "최악이 뭐겠어?" — 망설이는 상대를 가볍게 부추길 때 씀
expressions.nuance: 도전을 권유할 때 쓰는 표현. 코미디에서는 이 말 바로 뒤에 최악의 상황이 실제로 벌어지는 아이러니로 자주 쓰임. 이 에피소드도 그 패턴을 따름.
expressions.example: "Just send the email and ask for a raise. **What's the worst that could happen?**" → "그냥 이메일 보내서 연봉 인상 요청해봐. 최악이 뭐겠어?"

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Just go up to her and ask her out. (Chandler laughs) Oh, what's the worst thing that could happen?

expressions.dialogue:
[Monica and Rachel are fighting]
Phoebe: "Alright Monica, if there is something that you would like to share..."
Monica: "Ya know, you had no right to go out with him."
Rachel: "That is the most ridiculous—"
Monica: "You sold me out."
Rachel: "I did not sell you out." ...
Rachel: "Alright, I feel that this is totally unjustified. **She gave me the green light**, I did nothing but—"
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 누군가에게 허락이나 승인을 주다
expressions.nuance: 교통 신호등에서 온 표현. 공식적·비공식적 상황 모두에서 쓰임. "get the green light(승인을 받다)", "give the green light(승인을 주다)" 두 형태 모두 활용됨.
expressions.example: "The CEO finally **gave the green light** for the new project." → "CEO가 드디어 새 프로젝트를 승인했어."

expressions.dialogue:
[On the movie set, Chandler runs into an old classmate]
Susie: "I'm Susie Moss. Fourth grade, glasses, I used to carry around a box of animal crackers like a purse."
Chandler: "Susie Moss, right, yeah, wow, you look... **great job growing up**."
Susie: "It's nice to see you're not still wearing that denim cap with all the little mirrors on it."
Chandler: "Oh, right, well yeah, I graduated fourth grade and realized I wasn't a pimp."
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: "잘 자랐네" — 어색하게 말문이 막혔을 때 우회해서 쓰는 유머러스한 칭찬 표현
expressions.nuance: 문법적으로는 어색하지만("you look" 뒤에 말문이 막혀 우회한 것) 이 어색함 자체가 Chandler식 유머. 오랜만에 만난 사람이 훨씬 매력적으로 변했을 때 쓰는 표현으로, 실생활에서도 칭찬이 갑자기 막힐 때 유머로 활용 가능.
expressions.example: "I ran into my childhood friend at the airport. Wow... **great job growing up**." → "공항에서 어릴 때 친구를 우연히 만났는데. 와... 진짜 잘 자랐더라."

expressions.dialogue:
[At Monica and Rachel's, watching TV]
Joey On TV: "Damnit, I'm a doctor, I'm not God."
Ross: "**Well, there goes my whole belief system.**"
Erica: [on the intercom] "It's Erica."
Joey: "Oh my God, quick turn off the TV."
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: "~는 이제 끝이다", "~가 날아가 버렸네" — 믿음·계획·기대가 무너졌을 때 쓰는 표현
expressions.nuance: 진지하게도, 가볍게도 모두 쓸 수 있음. Ross의 개그는 Joey의 드라마 대사를 실제 의사의 말처럼 받아들이는 것. "there go my plans", "there goes that idea" 등으로 변형 가능.
expressions.example: "She's already taken? **There go my weekend plans**." → "그 사람 이미 사귀는 사람 있어? 주말 계획 다 날아갔다."

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Oh well, what do you know, there goes my identical twin sister. Just walkin' along looking like me. What, is this just like a freakish coincidence, or did you know she takes this train?

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Well, wait there's, there's more. See the contact paper is to go into your brand new drawer. (gives her a drawer) See, the drawer actually goes in my dresser.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: I go back there with lawn ornaments, he's going to laugh in my face.

expressions.dialogue:
Chandler: "Alright, one of you give me your underpants."
Joey: "Can't help you, I'm not wearing any."
Chandler: "How can you not be wearing any underwear?"
Joey: "Oh, **I'm gettin' heat from the guy in the hot pink thong.**"
Ross: [peering over the stall] "Hi Tushie."
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 누군가로부터 비판·압박·잔소리를 받다
expressions.nuance: 비공식적으로 비판이나 압박을 받는 상황에 쓰는 표현. "catch heat", "take heat"도 같은 의미. 직장·가족·친구 관계 등 다양한 맥락에서 쓰임.
expressions.example: "I've been **getting heat from** my parents about not calling home enough." → "부모님께 전화 자주 안 한다고 잔소리 엄청 듣고 있어."

expressions.dialogue:
Monica: "Ya know, you had no right to go out with him."
Rachel: "That is the most ridiculous—"
Monica: "**You sold me out.**"
Rachel: "I did not **sell you out**."
Monica: "Yes you did. Absolutely."
Rachel: "Would you let me talk." [flicks Monica on the forehead]
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 이익을 위해 상대를 배신하다, 팔아넘기다
expressions.nuance: "betray"보다 훨씬 구어적이고 감정적인 표현. 신뢰를 저버리고 상대방의 이익을 희생하는 행위에 씀. 비유적으로도, 실제 비밀을 누설하는 경우에도 사용.
expressions.example: "He **sold me out** to the teacher just to avoid getting in trouble himself." → "자기가 혼나지 않으려고 나를 선생님한테 팔아버렸어."

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: I did not sell you out.

occ.speaker: Lennart
occ.text: You making fun of me? Because I am not a sell-out. (He stands up and walks menacingly towards Joey) I didn't do that for the money, I believe in those phones. I almost lost a cousin because of bad wireless service.

expressions.dialogue:
[Central Perk, end of episode]
Chandler: "Hey Phoebs, can I have the milk after you?"
Phoebe: "I'm almost done with it, **keep your panties on**."
Joey: "Hey, hey, and I'm in the movie."
Ross: "What happened?"
Joey: "One of the virus victims called in sick, so Cathy recommended me and boom, I'm dying on the gurney."
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: 진정해, 서두르지 마
expressions.nuance: "keep your pants on"의 변형. 상대가 성급하게 구는 상황에서 "잠깐 기다려, 진정해"의 의미로 씀. 친한 사이에서만 써야 하며, 낯선 사람이나 어른에게 쓰면 무례할 수 있음.
expressions.example: "**Keep your panties on**, I'm almost ready to leave." → "진정해, 거의 다 됐어."

expressions.dialogue:
[At a restaurant, Erica is on a date with Joey, believing he's Dr. Drake Remore]
Erica: "Oh Drake, isn't it amazing? **Here we sit, devil may care**, just a little while ago you were reattaching someone's spinal cord."
Joey: "Yeah, that was a tricky one. In reality, that operation takes like, over 10 hours, but they only showed it for 2 minutes."
Erica: "Who's they?"
expressions.speaker: Erica
expressions.meaning: 결과 따위는 신경 쓰지 않고, 태평하게
expressions.nuance: 형용사(a devil-may-care attitude)로도, 부사적 표현으로도 쓰임. 다소 낭만적이거나 문학적인 느낌이 있어 일상보다는 약간 격식 있는 구어에서 등장하는 편.
expressions.example: "She quit her corporate job and traveled the world with a **devil-may-care** attitude." → "그녀는 회사를 그만두고 아무 걱정 없이 세계를 여행했어."

expressions.dialogue:
Susie: "Oh, shoot, we gotta go, got a reservation in 30 minutes."
Chandler: "Oh no no no no, no no no no, you see, **what I had planned shouldn't take more than 2, 3 minutes tops**."
Susie: "Oh, 200 seconds of passion. We gotta go."
Susie: "But um, here's an idea, have you ever worn women's underwear?"
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 기껏해야, 최대 — 숫자나 양 뒤에 붙여 "아무리 많아도"를 강조
expressions.nuance: 구어에서 수량 뒤에 붙여 최대치를 강조할 때 씀. "at most"보다 훨씬 캐주얼하고 자연스러운 표현. 문장 끝에 단독으로 오는 경우가 많음.
expressions.example: "The fix should take 10 minutes **tops**." → "수리는 아무리 길어도 10분이야."

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Yeah, she tops out at 130.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Remember?! We-come on both had the sarongs on, and we had the-the coconut bikini tops...

expressions.expression: use humor as a defense mechanism
expressions.dialogue:
Susie: "Remember the class play? You pulled up my skirt and the entire auditorium saw my underpants."
Chandler: "Yes, back then I, uh, **used humor as a defense mechanism**. Thank God I don't do that anymore." [immediately makes a self-deprecating joke]
Monica: "Oh my God, oh my God, oh my God."
Rachel: "What what what what?"
Monica: "Jean-Claude Van Damme. I didn't know he was in this movie, he is so hot."
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 불편하거나 어색한 상황을 웃음으로 넘기려 하다
expressions.nuance: 심리학 용어 "defense mechanism(방어기제)"을 일상 대화에서 가볍게 쓰는 예시. Chandler가 "이제 안 그런다"고 말하자마자 바로 농담을 하는 자기모순이 이 장면의 핵심 개그.
expressions.example: "My therapist thinks I **use humor as a defense mechanism** when I'm uncomfortable." → "치료사 말로는 내가 불편할 때 유머를 방어기제로 쓴다더라."

expressions.expression: How many times am I gonna have to X before you Y?
expressions.dialogue:
Susie: "Um, so listen, **how many times am I gonna have to touch you on the arm before you ask me on a date.**"
Chandler: "Well, uh, let's try one more... there you go, say Ernie's, 8 o'clock."
Susie: "I'll be there, and who knows, if things go well, maybe this time I'll get to see your underwear."
Chandler: "No one was around to hear that?"
expressions.speaker: Susie
expressions.meaning: "내가 몇 번이나 X를 해야 네가 Y를 할 거야?" — 힌트를 계속 줬는데 상대가 눈치채지 못할 때 쓰는 표현
expressions.nuance: 직접 요청하지 않고 상대가 먼저 움직여주길 기대하는 상황에서 쓰는, 약간 도발적이고 유머러스한 표현. 플러팅 상황뿐 아니라 반복적으로 뭔가를 요구할 때도 쓸 수 있음.
expressions.example: "**How many times am I gonna have to** bring this up before you actually fix it?" → "내가 이 문제를 몇 번이나 얘기해야 진짜로 고쳐줄 거야?"

expressions.expression: I don't think we should see each other anymore
expressions.dialogue:
Joey: "Alright look, that's it. **I don't think we should see each other anymore**, alright. Look, I know I should have told you this a long time ago but I am not Drake Remore, OK. I'm not even a doctor, I'm an actor."
Erica: "Oh my God. Do the people at the hospital know about this?"
Joey: "Somebody wanna help me out here?"
Rachel: "Oh, I know, I know." [Turns on the TV]
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 우리 더 이상 만나지 말자 — 이별을 완곡하게 선언하는 표현
expressions.nuance: "break up with you"보다 훨씬 부드럽고 어른스러운 이별 표현. "see each other"가 "사귀다/만나다"의 의미로 사용됨. 상대의 감정을 배려하는 어감이지만 의미는 명확함.
expressions.example: "After a few dates, she said **she didn't think we should see each other anymore**." → "몇 번 데이트한 후, 그녀가 더 이상 만나지 말자고 했어."

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Umm, wow. This-this isn't gonna be easy. Umm, I don't think we should see each other anymore.
